tasks.title: Create 10 social TS enums in packages/schemas/src/enums/
tasks.description:
Create one file per enum, following the pattern of existing enum files (e.g., `billing-interval.enum.ts`):
- `social-platform.enum.ts` → `SocialPlatform { INSTAGRAM, FACEBOOK, X }`
- `social-publish-format.enum.ts` → `SocialPublishFormat { FEED_POST, PHOTO_POST, TEXT_POST, IMAGE_POST, VIDEO_POST, REEL, STORY, CAROUSEL }`
- `social-media-type.enum.ts` → `SocialMediaType { IMAGE, VIDEO, NONE }`
- `social-post-status.enum.ts` → `SocialPostStatus { DRAFT, NEEDS_REVIEW, APPROVED, SCHEDULED, READY_TO_PUBLISH, PUBLISHING, PUBLISHED, FAILED, PAUSED, ARCHIVED }`
- `social-approval-status.enum.ts` → `SocialApprovalStatus { PENDING, APPROVED, REJECTED, CHANGES_REQUESTED }`
- `social-source.enum.ts` → `SocialSource { CHATGPT, ADMIN, IMPORT, SYSTEM }`
- `social-asset-source.enum.ts` → `SocialAssetSource { CHATGPT_FILE, CLOUDINARY, MANUAL_UPLOAD, EXTERNAL_URL }`
- `social-publish-result-status.enum.ts` → `SocialPublishResultStatus { SUCCESS, FAILED, SKIPPED, RETRYING }`
- `social-recurrence-type.enum.ts` → `SocialRecurrenceType { ONCE, WEEKLY, BIWEEKLY, MONTHLY }`
- `social-ai-request-status.enum.ts` → `SocialAiRequestStatus { PENDING, PROCESSED, FAILED }` (for social_ai_requests.status)
Each file exports the enum and a matching `z.nativeEnum()` schema in a co-located `social-<name>.schema.ts`. Use `as const` + JSDoc. All named exports.

Test requirements:
- Unit test file: `packages/schemas/src/enums/__tests__/social-enums.test.ts`
- Assert each enum has the expected members (Object.keys check)
- Assert each nativeEnum schema validates valid values and rejects invalid ones

tasks.title: Export social enums from packages/schemas/src/enums/index.ts
tasks.description:
Add barrel exports for all 10 new enum files and their schema files to `packages/schemas/src/enums/index.ts`. Follow the alphabetical ordering used for existing entries. Verify that importing `{ SocialPlatform, SocialPostStatus }` from `@repo/schemas` resolves correctly via `pnpm typecheck`.

Test requirements:
- No dedicated test file needed; covered by T-001 unit tests
- Verify with `pnpm typecheck` in `packages/schemas`

subtasks.title: Add 20 barrel export lines to enums/index.ts

tasks.title: Add 25 PermissionEnum + 9 PermissionCategoryEnum values for social domain
tasks.description:
Edit `packages/schemas/src/enums/permission.enum.ts` to append:
- 9 new `PermissionCategoryEnum` values: `SOCIAL_POST`, `SOCIAL_HASHTAG`, `SOCIAL_CAMPAIGN`, `SOCIAL_BATCH`, `SOCIAL_AUDIENCE`, `SOCIAL_PLATFORM`, `SOCIAL_FOOTER`, `SOCIAL_SETTINGS`, `SOCIAL_AUDIT`
- 25 new `PermissionEnum` values following the exact SCREAMING_SNAKE key + camelCase dot-value pattern, from `SOCIAL_POST_VIEW = 'socialPost.view'` through `SOCIAL_DISPATCH_MANAGE = 'socialDispatch.manage'` as listed in the spec. Add JSDoc comment per permission per the spec's risk mitigation note.

Test requirements:
- Unit test: `packages/schemas/src/enums/__tests__/permission.test.ts` (existing or new)
- Assert new enum keys exist and their string values match expected dot-notation
- Verify `pnpm typecheck` still passes (the enum is referenced in many places)

tasks.title: Register 10 social pgEnums in packages/db/src/schemas/enums.dbschema.ts
tasks.description:
For each of the 10 social TS enums, register a corresponding Drizzle pgEnum using `enumToTuple()` in `packages/db/src/schemas/enums.dbschema.ts`. Follow the pattern of existing pgEnums (e.g., `billingIntervalPgEnum`). Naming convention: `socialPlatformPgEnum`, `socialPublishFormatPgEnum`, etc. Export each from the same file. This file is referenced by DB table schema files.

Test requirements:
- No dedicated test; verified by `pnpm typecheck` in `packages/db`
- The generated migration (T-008) will confirm pgEnums are valid

tasks.title: Create DB table schemas for simple catalog tables (campaigns, batches, audiences, platforms, hashtag-sets, footers, assets)
tasks.description:
Create the following files in `packages/db/src/schemas/social/` (one file per table, each with a `relations()` block):
- `social-campaigns.dbschema.ts` — name, slug UNIQUE, description, active, starts_at, ends_at, plus standard audit columns
- `social-content-batches.dbschema.ts` — name, slug UNIQUE, description, active, starts_at, ends_at
- `social-audiences.dbschema.ts` — name, slug UNIQUE, description, active
- `social-platforms.dbschema.ts` — id UUID PK, platform socialPlatformPgEnum UNIQUE NOT NULL, label, enabled, notes (no plain enum PK per resolved decision #8 in spec)
- `social-hashtag-sets.dbschema.ts` — name, slug, platform nullable, hashtags_text, priority, active, notes
- `social-post-footers.dbschema.ts` — name, slug, content, platform nullable, active, is_default, priority, notes
- `social-assets.dbschema.ts` — source socialAssetSourcePgEnum, cloudinary_url, cloudinary_public_id, original_url nullable, openai_file_ref nullable, mime_type nullable, media_type socialMediaTypePgEnum, width, height, duration_seconds nullable, alt_text, caption, metadata_json (append-only, NO soft-delete columns)
All files: id UUID PK defaultRandom(), created_at, updated_at, plus audit FK columns (created_by_id, updated_by_id, deleted_at, deleted_by_id) except social_assets (append-only).

Test requirements:
- No dedicated unit test; schema correctness verified by migration generation in T-008
- `pnpm typecheck` in `packages/db`

tasks.title: Create DB table schemas for hashtags, platform-formats, and settings tables
tasks.description:
Create in `packages/db/src/schemas/social/`:
- `social-hashtags.dbschema.ts` — hashtag, normalized_hashtag UNIQUE, category, platform nullable (socialPlatformPgEnum), audience_id FK→social_audiences nullable, priority, active, notes; plus standard audit columns
- `social-platform-formats.dbschema.ts` — platform socialPlatformPgEnum, publish_format socialPublishFormatPgEnum, media_type socialMediaTypePgEnum, enabled, mvp_enabled, recommended_ratio nullable, recommended_size nullable, max_caption_length, requires_public_url bool, requires_media bool, make_channel_key nullable, notes; UNIQUE(platform, publish_format); plus standard audit columns
- `social-settings.dbschema.ts` — key UNIQUE, value text, type text, active bool, description; plus standard audit columns

Test requirements:
- No dedicated unit test; schema correctness verified by migration generation in T-008
- `pnpm typecheck` in `packages/db`

tasks.title: Create DB table schemas for social_posts, social_post_targets, social_post_media, social_post_hashtags
tasks.description:
Create in `packages/db/src/schemas/social/`:
- `social-posts.dbschema.ts` — all columns per spec data model: draft_id UNIQUE, title, slug, source, pillar, campaign_id FK, batch_id FK, batch_position nullable, audience_id FK, footer_id FK, base_hashtag_set_id FK, caption_base, final_caption, final_hashtags_text, status socialPostStatusPgEnum, approval_status socialApprovalStatusPgEnum, paused bool default false, retry_count int default 0, scheduled_at nullable, timezone, recurrence_type socialRecurrenceTypePgEnum default ONCE, recurrence_params_json nullable, next_run_at nullable, notes, internal_notes, gpt_hashtag_payload_json, metadata_json, approved_by_id FK nullable, approved_at nullable; plus standard audit columns
- `social-post-targets.dbschema.ts` — social_post_id FK, platform, publish_format, media_type, platform_format_id FK, caption_override nullable, hashtags_override_text nullable, footer_override nullable, status socialPostStatusPgEnum (target-level), scheduled_at nullable, published_at nullable, external_post_id nullable, external_post_url nullable, make_scenario_key nullable, make_last_run_id nullable, make_payload_json nullable, last_error_message nullable, retry_count int default 0; plus standard audit columns
- `social-post-media.dbschema.ts` — social_post_id FK, asset_id FK→social_assets, position int; UNIQUE(social_post_id, position)
- `social-post-hashtags.dbschema.ts` — social_post_id FK, hashtag_id FK→social_hashtags, position int; UNIQUE(social_post_id, hashtag_id)
All include `relations()` block.

Test requirements:
- Schema correctness verified by T-008 migration generation
- `pnpm typecheck`

tasks.title: Create DB table schemas for append-only tables (social_ai_requests, social_publish_logs, social_audit_log)
tasks.description:
Create in `packages/db/src/schemas/social/`:
- `social-ai-requests.dbschema.ts` — request_id UNIQUE, source socialSourcePgEnum, topic, user_idea, pillar, audience_id FK nullable→social_audiences, suggested_platforms_json, suggested_format, generated_caption_base, raw_request_json, raw_response_json, status socialAiRequestStatusPgEnum, error_message; created_at, updated_at only (NO soft-delete, NO updated_by/deleted_by per append-only spec)
- `social-publish-logs.dbschema.ts` — social_post_id FK, social_post_target_id FK nullable, platform nullable, publish_format nullable, status socialPublishResultStatusPgEnum, message, request_payload_json, response_payload_json, external_post_id nullable, external_post_url nullable, make_run_id nullable; created_at only (append-only)
- `social-audit-log.dbschema.ts` — actor_id UUID nullable, event_type text, entity_type text, entity_id text, old_value_json jsonb nullable, new_value_json jsonb nullable, metadata_json jsonb nullable; created_at only (append-only)

Test requirements:
- Schema correctness verified by migration generation
- `pnpm typecheck`

tasks.title: Export all 17 social table schemas from packages/db/src/schemas/index.ts
tasks.description:
Add barrel exports for all 17 new social table schema files to `packages/db/src/schemas/index.ts`. Also ensure the social domain directory has its own `index.ts` that re-exports all 17 files, and that file is imported from the root schema index. Follow the pattern of existing domains (e.g., `accommodation/`).

tasks.title: Generate migration file 0022_social_automation.sql via pnpm db:generate
tasks.description:
Run `pnpm db:generate` from the worktree root to produce `packages/db/src/migrations/0022_social_automation.sql`. Verify the output contains:
- All 10 new pgEnum CREATE TYPE statements
- All 17 CREATE TABLE statements
- The correct FK references between tables
- No unexpected drops or alterations to existing tables

If the generator creates an unexpected file number (e.g., `0023_...`), check that the previous migration was committed. Commit the generated SQL file as part of the PR.

Test requirements:
- Run `pnpm db:migrate` against a test/dev database; confirm 0 errors
- Run `pnpm typecheck` in `packages/db` after migration

tasks.title: Create DB model files for all 17 social tables in packages/db/src/models/social/
tasks.description:
Create one BaseModelImpl-extending model file per table in `packages/db/src/models/social/`. Each model provides typed query methods (findById, findMany, create, update, softDelete) following the pattern of existing models (e.g., `packages/db/src/models/accommodation/`). The append-only tables (social_ai_requests, social_publish_logs, social_audit_log) only expose `create` and `findMany` — no update/softDelete.

Split into 3 atomic batches:
- Batch A: simple catalog models (campaigns, batches, audiences, platforms, hashtag-sets, footers, hashtags, platform-formats, settings)
- Batch B: social_posts + social_post_targets models
- Batch C: join-table models (social_post_media, social_post_hashtags, social_assets) + append-only models (social_ai_requests, social_publish_logs, social_audit_log)

Also create `packages/db/src/models/social/index.ts` and export from `packages/db/src/models/index.ts`.

Test requirements:
- Unit tests for non-trivial query methods (soft delete excludes deleted_at records, findMany with filters)
- File: `packages/db/src/models/social/__tests__/social-models.test.ts`

subtasks.title: Create model index and register in packages/db/src/models/index.ts

tasks.title: Create Zod entity schema directories for all 17 social entities in packages/schemas/src/entities/social/
tasks.description:
For each of the 17 social tables, create a directory under `packages/schemas/src/entities/social/<entity>/` containing these files (following the existing entity schema pattern, e.g., `packages/schemas/src/entities/accommodation/`):
- `<entity>.schema.ts` — base Zod shape mirroring the DB row
- `<entity>.crud.schema.ts` — CreateSchema + UpdateSchema (partial)
- `<entity>.query.schema.ts` — ListQuerySchema (page, pageSize, search, filters)
- `<entity>.http.schema.ts` — HTTP request/response wrappers
- `<entity>.access.schema.ts` — access-control shape
- `index.ts` — re-exports all schemas

Key schemas requiring extra fields:
- `social-post.crud.schema.ts`: `CreateSocialDraftSchema` (the full GPT payload per the spec's API Design section), `UpdateSocialPostSchema` (partial, excludes state fields)
- `social-post.http.schema.ts`: response envelope matching `GET /admin/social/posts/:id` nested structure

Also create `packages/schemas/src/entities/social/index.ts` and add it to the root `packages/schemas/src/entities/index.ts`.

Test requirements:
- Unit tests for complex schemas: `CreateSocialDraftSchema` (required fields, optional fields, nested targets array min 1)
- File: `packages/schemas/src/entities/social/__tests__/social-schemas.test.ts`

subtasks.title: Create entity schema dirs for 9 catalog entities
Create entity schema dirs for social_posts + social_post_targets
Create entity schema dirs for join-table + append-only entities

tasks.title: Create seed data files for all catalog entities (platforms, platform-formats, campaigns, batches, audiences, hashtag-sets, hashtags, footers, settings)

- `social-campaigns.seed.ts` (at least 1 default row: `institucional-hospeda`)

tasks.title: Create catalog CRUD services for hashtag, hashtag-set, footer, campaign, batch, audience
tasks.description:
Create 6 service modules in `packages/service-core/src/services/social/`, each with:
- `<entity>.service.ts` — extends BaseCrudService; implements list, getById, create, update, softDelete. Uses Result<T> return type.
- `<entity>.permissions.ts` — maps CRUD operations to the relevant PermissionEnum values (from T-003)
- `<entity>.normalizers.ts` — e.g., hashtag normalization: toLowerCase + ensure `#` prefix for `normalized_hashtag`

Special behavior per entity:
- HashtagService: normalize to lowercase + `#` prefix, check uniqueness of `normalized_hashtag`, return CONFLICT if duplicate
- HashtagSetService: standard CRUD
- FooterService: standard CRUD
- CampaignService: standard CRUD
- BatchService: standard CRUD
- AudienceService: standard CRUD

All list methods use `safeIlike()` for search (never raw `ilike()`). All support `page`+`pageSize` pagination.

Test requirements:
- Unit tests for HashtagService: normalization (`#playa` → `#playa`, `playa` → `#playa`), uniqueness conflict, softDelete, list with search
- Unit tests for other services: create, update, softDelete, list pagination
- File: `packages/service-core/src/services/social/__tests__/catalog-services.test.ts`

tasks.title: Create PlatformFormatService and SettingsService
tasks.description:
Create 2 service modules in `packages/service-core/src/services/social/`:

`platform-format.service.ts`:
- `list()` — returns all rows (active + inactive) ordered by platform ASC, publish_format ASC
- `update(id, data)` — updates enabled, mvpEnabled, maxCaptionLength, makeChannelKey, notes, recommendedRatio, recommendedSize; checks how many active social_post_targets reference this format and returns warning count in the result if enabled is being set to false
- Permissions: `SOCIAL_PLATFORM_FORMAT_VIEW` (list), `SOCIAL_PLATFORM_MANAGE` (update)

`settings.service.ts`:
- `listAll()` — returns all settings rows; masks `type = 'secret'` values as `'***'`
- `getByKey(key)` — 404 if key not found
- `update(key, value)` — 404 if key not found; logs audit event SETTING_UPDATED with old/new values (secrets redacted in log)
- Permissions: `SOCIAL_SETTINGS_MANAGE`

Test requirements:
- Unit tests for SettingsService: secret masking on listAll, audit log call on update, 404 on unknown key
- Unit tests for PlatformFormatService: update returns warning count when disabling a format with active targets
- File: `packages/service-core/src/services/social/__tests__/platform-format-settings.test.ts`

tasks.title: Wire catalog admin API routes for hashtags, hashtag-sets, footers, campaigns, batches, audiences
tasks.description:
Create route files in `apps/api/src/routes/admin/social/` for each of the 6 catalog entities:
- `hashtags.route.ts`: GET /list, POST /create, GET /:id, PATCH /:id, DELETE /:id
- `hashtag-sets.route.ts`: same 5 endpoints
- `footers.route.ts`: same 5 endpoints
- `campaigns.route.ts`: same 5 endpoints
- `batches.route.ts`: same 5 endpoints
- `audiences.route.ts`: same 5 endpoints

Each route:
- Uses `createAdminRoute` (or equivalent admin route factory)
- Imports `requiredPermissions` from the service's `.permissions.ts`
- Calls the corresponding service method
- Returns `ResponseFactory` envelope
- List endpoints: validate query params with Zod (page, pageSize, search)
- POST: returns HTTP 201; PATCH/DELETE: HTTP 200

Register all 6 route files under the `/api/v1/admin/social` prefix in the API router.

Test requirements:
- Integration tests for each route file: 403 on missing permission, 201 on create, 409 on duplicate hashtag, 200 on list with search filter
- Files: `apps/api/src/__tests__/routes/admin/social/catalog-routes.test.ts`

tasks.title: Wire platform-formats and settings admin API routes
tasks.description:
Create route files in `apps/api/src/routes/admin/social/`:

`platform-formats.route.ts`:
- `GET /` — permission `SOCIAL_PLATFORM_FORMAT_VIEW`, calls `PlatformFormatService.list()`
- `PATCH /:id` — permission `SOCIAL_PLATFORM_MANAGE`, calls `PlatformFormatService.update()`, includes warnings in response if active targets reference the format

`settings.route.ts`:
- `GET /` — permission `SOCIAL_SETTINGS_MANAGE`, calls `SettingsService.listAll()` (secrets masked)
- `PATCH /:key` — permission `SOCIAL_SETTINGS_MANAGE`, calls `SettingsService.update()`, 404 on unknown key

Register both under `/api/v1/admin/social`.

Test requirements:
- Integration tests: GET settings returns masked secrets, PATCH settings 404 on unknown key, PATCH platform-format warns on active targets
- File: `apps/api/src/__tests__/routes/admin/social/platform-settings-routes.test.ts`

subtasks.title: Register routes and write integration tests

Test requirements:

tasks.title: Build admin UI pages for platform-formats and settings
tasks.description:
Create TanStack Start route files in `apps/admin/src/routes/_authed/social/`:
- `platform-formats/index.tsx` — DataTable with columns: platform, publishFormat, mediaType, enabled, mvpEnabled, maxCaptionLength, makeChannelKey; Edit action (no delete, no create per resolved decision); shows warning in edit modal if disabling a format with active targets
- `settings/index.tsx` — Key-value table with columns: key, type (badge), value (masked if secret), description; Edit action opens inline edit for value field only; no create/delete (all settings are seeded)

Both pages: TanStack Query, Shadcn DataTable, permission-gated, i18n labels, loading skeleton, error states.

Test requirements:

- File: `apps/admin/src/routes/_authed/social/__tests__/platform-settings-pages.test.tsx`

subtasks.title: Add es locale keys for all Phase 1 pages

tasks.title: Add env vars HOSPEDA_AI_SOCIAL_KEY and HOSPEDA_OPERATOR_PIN_HASH
tasks.description:
Following the CLAUDE.md env-var workflow (all 5 steps):
1. Register both vars in `packages/config/src/env-registry.*.ts` with metadata: type=secret, required=true, apps=['api'], category='social'
2. Add Zod validation in `apps/api/src/utils/env.ts` (extend ApiEnvBaseSchema)
3. Update `apps/api/.env.example` with safe placeholder values
4. Document purpose in the API app's CLAUDE.md or env guide
5. (Inform user to set in Coolify — do NOT block the task on this)

For `HOSPEDA_OPERATOR_PIN_HASH`: document that it stores `sha256(pin + secret_salt)` and the salt is `HOSPEDA_AI_SOCIAL_KEY` (or a separate salt var — clarify with owner). The raw PIN is never persisted per resolved decision #1.

Test requirements:
- Unit test: env schema validation rejects startup when `HOSPEDA_AI_SOCIAL_KEY` is missing
- File: `apps/api/src/__tests__/env.test.ts` (extend existing)

tasks.title: Create inbound API-key middleware factory (apps/api/src/middlewares/api-key.ts)
tasks.description:
Create `apps/api/src/middlewares/api-key.ts`. It exports a factory function:
```ts
createApiKeyMiddleware(config: { headerName: string; envVarKey: string; errorCode: string }): MiddlewareHandler
```
The returned middleware:
1. Reads `config.headerName` from the incoming request headers
2. Reads `process.env[config.envVarKey]` for the expected value
3. Compares using `crypto.timingSafeEqual(Buffer.from(incoming), Buffer.from(expected))`
4. On mismatch: logs WARN with `{ event: 'api_key_mismatch', header: config.headerName }` (no key value in log), returns HTTP 401 `{ success: false, error: { code: config.errorCode, message: '...' } }`
5. On match: injects synthetic actor `{ id: 'api-key', role: 'SYSTEM' }` into `c.set('actor', ...)` so downstream logging hooks work without crashing

Test requirements:
- Unit tests: valid key → passes through, invalid key → 401, missing header → 401, timingSafeEqual prevents timing attack (use fixed-time mock)
- File: `apps/api/src/__tests__/middlewares/api-key.test.ts`

subtasks.title: Handle length-mismatch edge case (timingSafeEqual requires equal buffer length)

tasks.title: Add createApiKeyRoute factory to route-factory-tiered.ts
tasks.description:
Add `createApiKeyRoute` to `apps/api/src/utils/route-factory-tiered.ts`. It mirrors `createAdminRoute` but:
- Swaps `adminAuthMiddleware` for the `createApiKeyMiddleware` instance
- Accepts `apiKeyConfig: { headerName: string; envVarKey: string; errorCode: string }` as a required option
- Applies rate limiting appropriate for AI/external inbound routes

Export the new factory alongside the existing ones. Existing factories must not be modified.

Test requirements:
- Unit test: route created with `createApiKeyRoute` rejects requests without the configured header

Handler logic:

- Fetch `social_settings` for defaults (timezone, campaignSlug, batchSlug, maxHashtagsPerPlatform)

- Apply `active = true` filter to every collection (soft-deleted records excluded)

tasks.title: Create SocialImagePipelineService
tasks.description:
Create `packages/service-core/src/services/social/social-image-pipeline.service.ts`.

Interface:
```ts
pipelineUpload({ mode, imagePayload, postId, altText?, mimeType? }): Promise<ImagePipelineResult>
```

Logic:
1. Detect mode: `public_url` → extract `url`; `openai_file_refs` → iterate array extracting each `downloadUrl`
2. For each download URL: `fetch(url, { signal: AbortSignal.timeout(15_000) })` (15s timeout per resolved decision #2)
3. Upload to Cloudinary via `getMediaProvider().upload(stream, { folder: 'social', resourceType: 'image' })`
4. On success: create `social_assets` row (cloudinary_url, cloudinary_public_id, original_url, width, height from upload result)
5. On failure (download timeout or Cloudinary error): log WARN; return `{ status: 'failed', warning: 'Media upload failed; manual upload required' }`; do NOT throw (draft creation continues per spec)
6. Create `social_post_media` join row linking postId to the new asset

Also add `duration_seconds?: number` to `UploadResult` type in `packages/media/src/types.ts`.

Test requirements:
- Unit tests: public_url mode success, openai_file_refs mode (array), download timeout → warning returned, Cloudinary failure → warning returned, post-media row created on success
- File: `packages/service-core/src/services/social/__tests__/social-image-pipeline.test.ts`

subtasks.title: Create service file with mode detection and download logic
Implement Cloudinary upload + social_assets + social_post_media writes

tasks.title: Create SocialDraftIngestionService
tasks.description:
Create `packages/service-core/src/services/social/social-draft-ingestion.service.ts`.

Method: `ingestDraft(payload: CreateSocialDraftSchema, actorId: string): Promise<IngestDraftResult>`

Steps:
1. Validate `operator_pin`: hash the incoming pin using `sha256(pin + env.HOSPEDA_OPERATOR_PIN_HASH_SALT)` and compare with `timingSafeEqual` against `env.HOSPEDA_OPERATOR_PIN_HASH`. Return 403 on mismatch.
2. Check `draft_id` uniqueness in `social_posts`; return 409 on conflict.
3. Resolve slugs → IDs: campaign, batch, audience, footer, base_hashtag_set (log warnings for unresolved, do not block)
4. Validate targets: for each `{platform, publishFormat}`, check an active `social_platform_formats` row exists. Collect invalid targets as warnings. If ALL targets invalid → return 422.
5. Override: force `status = NEEDS_REVIEW`, `approval_status = PENDING`, `paused = false` regardless of what the payload specifies. Add warnings if payload tried to set state fields.
6. Write `social_posts` row (DB transaction).
7. Write `social_post_targets` rows for each valid target.
8. Process `curatedHashtags`: look up each by `normalized_hashtag` in `social_hashtags`; create `social_post_hashtags` for found ones; add warnings for unresolved.
9. Store `customHashtagSuggestions` verbatim in `social_posts.gpt_hashtag_payload_json` — do NOT add to `social_post_hashtags`.
10. Write `social_ai_requests` row (append-only log).
11. Trigger `SocialImagePipelineService.pipelineUpload()` if `image` is present. Collect media warnings.
12. Return `{ postId, draftId, status, approvalStatus, targetsCreated, assetStatus, warnings }`.

Test requirements:
- Unit tests covering all 12 steps; focus on: operator_pin mismatch → 403, draft_id duplicate → 409, all-targets-invalid → 422, status override with warnings, unknown curatedHashtags → warnings not errors, image failure → draft still created
- File: `packages/service-core/src/services/social/__tests__/social-draft-ingestion.test.ts`

subtasks.title: Implement steps 1-5 (validation, pin check, slug resolution, target validation, override)
Implement steps 6-10 (DB writes in transaction, hashtag linkage)

tasks.description:
Create `apps/api/src/routes/ai/social/drafts.route.ts` using `createApiKeyRoute` with the AI social key config.

Handler:
1. Parse body with `CreateSocialDraftSchema` from `@repo/schemas` — return 422 on validation failure
2. Call `SocialDraftIngestionService.ingestDraft(payload, 'api-key')`
3. Map service result to HTTP response:
   - Success → HTTP 201, `{ success: true, data: { postId, draftId, status, approvalStatus, targetsCreated, assetStatus, warnings } }`
   - 403 pin mismatch → HTTP 403
   - 409 conflict → HTTP 409
   - 422 no valid targets → HTTP 422
4. Register under `/api/v1/ai/social/drafts`

Test requirements:
- Integration tests: valid payload → 201, missing required fields → 422, duplicate draftId → 409, wrong pin → 403, no valid targets → 422
- File: `apps/api/src/__tests__/routes/ai/social/drafts.test.ts`

tasks.title: Implement GET /api/v1/admin/social/gpt-action-schema route
tasks.description:
Create `apps/api/src/routes/admin/social/gpt-action-schema.route.ts`.

Auth: `createAdminRoute` with `requiredPermissions: [SOCIAL_SETTINGS_MANAGE]`.

Handler generates programmatically (not hand-authored) an OpenAPI 3.1 JSON document containing exactly 2 paths:
1. `GET /api/v1/ai/social/catalog` — operationId `getSocialCatalog`; request: no body, header `x-hospeda-ai-key`; response: catalog response schema derived from Zod schema
2. `POST /api/v1/ai/social/drafts` — operationId `saveSocialDraft`; request body: `CreateSocialDraftSchema`; response: draft ingestion response schema

Use `zod-to-json-schema` (if already in the project) or construct the OpenAPI shape manually from the Zod `.shape` property. Return `Content-Type: application/json`. Works in production (not guarded by NODE_ENV).

Register under `/api/v1/admin/social/gpt-action-schema`.

Test requirements:
- Unit test: response is valid JSON, contains exactly 2 paths with the specified operationIds
- File: `apps/api/src/__tests__/routes/admin/social/gpt-action-schema.test.ts`

tasks.title: Create SocialAuditLogService
Create SocialPostService — core state-transition methods (approve, reject, requestChanges)
tasks.description:
Create `packages/service-core/src/services/social/social-post.service.ts` (non-CRUD, does not extend BaseCrudService).

Implement first batch of methods:

`approve(postId, actorId)`:
- Load post; assert status === NEEDS_REVIEW → 422 if not
- Assert at least 1 active target exists → 422 if not (zero active targets block)
- Check if any target requires media (`requires_media = true` on platform_format): if post has no social_post_media rows → 422 MISSING_MEDIA
- Set status=APPROVED, approval_status=APPROVED, approved_by_id=actorId, approved_at=now() (atomic DB update)
- Call `SocialAuditLogService.log(POST_APPROVED, old/new values)`
- Return `{ id, status, approvalStatus, approvedAt }`

`reject(postId, actorId, reason)`:
- Assert reason is non-empty → 422 if blank
- Assert post is in NEEDS_REVIEW → 422 if not
- Set approval_status=REJECTED, append reason to notes
- Log POST_REJECTED

`requestChanges(postId, actorId, feedback)`:
- Assert feedback is non-empty → 422
- Set approval_status=CHANGES_REQUESTED, append feedback to notes
- Log POST_CHANGES_REQUESTED

Test requirements:
- Unit tests: approve succeeds, approve fails on wrong status, approve fails on missing media, reject requires reason, requestChanges requires feedback
- File: `packages/service-core/src/services/social/__tests__/social-post-service-approve.test.ts`

tasks.title: Extend SocialPostService — scheduling, markReady, pause, unpause, archive
tasks.description:
Add to `social-post.service.ts`:

`schedule(postId, actorId, { scheduledAt, timezone, recurrenceType?, recurrenceParamsJson? })`:
- Assert post is APPROVED or already SCHEDULED
- Assert scheduledAt is in the future → 422 if past
- If recurrenceType is WEEKLY, assert recurrenceParamsJson.weekday is valid SocialWeekday → 422 if missing
- Set status=SCHEDULED, scheduled_at, timezone, next_run_at=scheduled_at, recurrence fields
- Log POST_SCHEDULED (or POST_RESCHEDULED if was already SCHEDULED, with old/new scheduledAt)

`markReady(postId, actorId)`:
- Assert post is APPROVED
- Set status=READY_TO_PUBLISH, next_run_at=now()
- Log POST_MARKED_READY

`pause(postId, actorId)`:
- Assert status in (APPROVED, SCHEDULED, READY_TO_PUBLISH) and paused=false; else 422
- Assert status not in (PUBLISHED, FAILED) → 422
- Set paused=true; log POST_PAUSED

`unpause(postId, actorId)`:
- Assert paused=true → 422 if not paused
- Set paused=false; log POST_UNPAUSED

`archive(postId, actorId)`:
- Assert status !== PUBLISHING → 422 if in PUBLISHING
- Set status=ARCHIVED, deleted_at=now()
- Log POST_ARCHIVED

Test requirements:
- Unit tests: schedule rejects past dates, schedule WEEKLY without weekday → 422, markReady requires APPROVED, pause rejects PUBLISHED/FAILED, archive blocks on PUBLISHING
- File: `packages/service-core/src/services/social/__tests__/social-post-service-state.test.ts`

tasks.title: Extend SocialPostService — list, getDetail, updatePost, promoteHashtag

`listPosts(query)` — paginated list with filters (status, approvalStatus, platform, search, createdAtFrom/To, includeDeleted). Search uses `safeIlike()`. If includeDeleted=false (default), excludes deleted_at IS NOT NULL. Returns items with: id, title, slug, status, approvalStatus, paused, targets (platforms), thumbnailUrl (first media), scheduledAt, createdAt.

`getPostDetail(postId)` — full post with nested targets, media (Cloudinary URLs), hashtags (resolved text), last 10 publishLogs, gptHashtagPayloadJson.

`updatePost(postId, actorId, data)` — partial update of title, captionBase, finalCaption, finalHashtagsText, notes, internalNotes, footerId, campaignId, batchId, audienceId, batchPosition. Blocks update of state fields (status, approvalStatus, paused).

`promoteHashtag(postId, actorId, { hashtag, category, platform?, audienceId?, priority? })`:
- Assert post exists
- Auto-prepend `#` if missing (add to warnings)
- Normalize to lowercase
- Upsert in `social_hashtags` (create if not exists by normalized_hashtag)
- Upsert in `social_post_hashtags` (create if not exists for this post+hashtag)
- Log HASHTAG_PROMOTED
- Return `{ hashtagId, hashtag, isNew }`, HTTP 200 if existing, 201 if new

Test requirements:
- Unit tests: listPosts pagination and filter, includeDeleted=false excludes archived, updatePost blocks state fields, promoteHashtag idempotency (same hashtag twice → no duplicate), promoteHashtag auto-prepends #
- File: `packages/service-core/src/services/social/__tests__/social-post-service-list.test.ts`

tasks.title: Create SocialPublishDispatchService — findEligibleTargets and buildMakePayload
tasks.description:
Create `packages/service-core/src/services/social/social-publish-dispatch.service.ts`.

Implement first two methods:

`findEligibleTargets(): Promise<EligibleTarget[]>`:
Query `social_post_targets` joining `social_posts` where:
- `social_posts.approval_status = APPROVED`
- `social_posts.status = READY_TO_PUBLISH` OR (`social_posts.status = SCHEDULED` AND `social_posts.next_run_at <= now()`)
- `social_posts.paused = false`
- `social_post_targets.status` NOT IN (PUBLISHED, FAILED, PUBLISHING)
- Parent post has at least 1 `social_post_media` row
Return fully-typed array with post + target data needed for dispatch.

`buildMakePayload(target, post): MakeDispatchPayload`:
Build the outbound payload per spec:
```ts
{ targetId, postId, platform, publishFormat, makeChannelKey, captionFinal, hashtagsFinal, footerFinal, mediaUrls: [...], scheduledAt, timezone, callbackClaimUrl, callbackResultUrl }
```
`callbackClaimUrl` = `${env.HOSPEDA_API_URL}/api/v1/integrations/make/social/jobs/${target.id}/claim`
`callbackResultUrl` = `${env.HOSPEDA_API_URL}/api/v1/integrations/make/social/jobs/${target.id}/result`

Test requirements:
- Unit tests: findEligibleTargets excludes paused posts, excludes posts with no media, excludes already-PUBLISHED targets, excludes PUBLISHING targets (optimistic lock)
- Unit tests: buildMakePayload correctly assembles all payload fields including callback URLs
- File: `packages/service-core/src/services/social/__tests__/dispatch-find-build.test.ts`

subtasks.title: Implement findEligibleTargets() with all filter conditions

tasks.title: Implement SocialPublishDispatchService — dispatchTarget with retry logic
tasks.description:
Add to `social-publish-dispatch.service.ts`:

`dispatchTarget(targetId: string): Promise<DispatchResult>`:
1. Load target + parent post
2. Set target.status = PUBLISHING (optimistic lock, atomic DB update)
3. Read `make_webhook_url` from `social_settings` (NOT from env/cache — live read per spec)
4. POST to webhook URL with `buildMakePayload()` result and header `x-make-apikey: env.HOSPEDA_MAKE_API_KEY`. Timeout: 30s.
5. On HTTP 2xx: insert `social_publish_logs` row with status=RETRYING. Set post.status=PUBLISHING if not already. Return success.
6. On failure (non-2xx or network error):
   a. If target.retry_count < 3: increment retry_count, reset target.status=APPROVED (will be retried next cron run), insert publish_log with status=FAILED + error message. Return `{ retrying: true }`.
   b. If target.retry_count >= 3: set target.status=FAILED, insert publish_log with status=FAILED, log audit event TARGET_DISPATCH_FAILED_EXHAUSTED. Call `cascadePostStatus(postId)`. Return `{ failed: true }`.

Test requirements:
- Unit tests: dispatch success → target PUBLISHING, log RETRYING; dispatch failure retry_count < 3 → target back to APPROVED, retry_count incremented; dispatch failure retry_count >= 3 → target FAILED, audit logged
- File: `packages/service-core/src/services/social/__tests__/dispatch-target.test.ts`

subtasks.title: Implement outbound webhook POST with header
Implement success path (log RETRYING, set post PUBLISHING)

tasks.title: Implement SocialPublishDispatchService — cascadePostStatus and rearmRecurrence
tasks.description:
Add to `social-publish-dispatch.service.ts`:

`cascadePostStatus(postId: string): Promise<void>`:
- Load all targets for the post
- If all targets are in terminal state (PUBLISHED, FAILED, SKIPPED):
  - If at least 1 target is PUBLISHED → set post.status = PUBLISHED
  - If ALL targets are FAILED or SKIPPED → set post.status = FAILED
  - If status becomes PUBLISHED → call `rearmRecurrence(post)`
  - If status becomes FAILED → set next_run_at = null (no rearm on failure per resolved decision)

`rearmRecurrence(post): Promise<void>`:
- If recurrence_type = ONCE → set next_run_at = null. Leave status = PUBLISHED.
- If recurrence_type = WEEKLY → compute next occurrence (next weekday from recurrence_params_json.weekday at or after now() in post.timezone), set next_run_at, reset status = APPROVED
- If recurrence_type = BIWEEKLY → next_run_at = now() + 14 days (adjusted to same weekday), reset status = APPROVED
- If recurrence_type = MONTHLY → next_run_at = same day next month, reset status = APPROVED
- When rearming: reset ALL targets to status=APPROVED AND retry_count=0 (resolved decision #3 — clean slate every cycle)

Test requirements:
- Unit tests: partial success (1 PUBLISHED + 1 FAILED) → post=PUBLISHED, ONCE → next_run_at null, WEEKLY → correct next Monday, MONTHLY → same day next month, rearm resets all target retry_count to 0
- File: `packages/service-core/src/services/social/__tests__/dispatch-cascade-rearm.test.ts`

Implement target reset (status=APPROVED, retry_count=0) on rearm
Write unit tests for all recurrence types and cascade edge cases

tasks.title: Implement SocialPublishDispatchService — handleMakeCallbackClaim and handleMakeCallbackResult
tasks.description:
Add to `social-publish-dispatch.service.ts`:

`handleMakeCallbackClaim(targetId: string, makeRunId: string): Promise<ClaimResult>`:
- Load target; 404 if not found
- If target.status = PUBLISHED → return 409 ALREADY_PUBLISHED
- Set target.status = PUBLISHING, target.make_last_run_id = makeRunId, update make_payload_json
- Return `{ targetId, status: 'PUBLISHING' }`

`handleMakeCallbackResult(targetId: string, result: MakeResultPayload): Promise<ResultCallbackResult>`:
- Load target; 404 if not found
- If result.status = 'SUCCESS':
  - Set target.status = PUBLISHED, published_at = now(), external_post_id, external_post_url
  - Insert publish_log with status = SUCCESS
  - Log audit event TARGET_PUBLISHED
- If result.status = 'FAILED':
  - Store last_error_message
  - If target.retry_count < 3: reset target.status to pre-PUBLISHING state (READY_TO_PUBLISH or APPROVED), increment retry_count
  - If retry_count >= 3: set target.status = FAILED
  - Insert publish_log with status = FAILED
  - Log audit event TARGET_PUBLISH_FAILED
- Call `cascadePostStatus(target.social_post_id)` in both cases
- Return final state

Test requirements:
- Unit tests: claim sets PUBLISHING, claim on PUBLISHED → 409, result SUCCESS cascades, result FAILED with retry_count < 3 → resets to pre-PUBLISHING, result FAILED with retry_count >= 3 → stays FAILED
- File: `packages/service-core/src/services/social/__tests__/dispatch-callbacks.test.ts`

subtasks.title: Implement handleMakeCallbackResult() FAILED path with retry logic

tasks.title: Implement Make callback routes (claim and result)
tasks.description:
Create route files in `apps/api/src/routes/integrations/make/social/`:

`claim.route.ts` — POST /api/v1/integrations/make/social/jobs/:targetId/claim:
- Use `createApiKeyRoute` with `{ headerName: 'x-hospeda-make-key', envVarKey: 'HOSPEDA_MAKE_INBOUND_KEY', errorCode: 'UNAUTHORIZED' }`
- Body: `{ makeRunId: string }` (Zod-validated)
- Calls `SocialPublishDispatchService.handleMakeCallbackClaim(targetId, makeRunId)`
- Maps: 404 → HTTP 404, 409 ALREADY_PUBLISHED → HTTP 409, success → HTTP 200

`result.route.ts` — POST /api/v1/integrations/make/social/jobs/:targetId/result:
- Same Make key middleware
- Body: `{ makeRunId, status: 'SUCCESS'|'FAILED', externalPostId?, externalPostUrl?, errorMessage? }` (Zod-validated, status is required)
- Calls `SocialPublishDispatchService.handleMakeCallbackResult(targetId, result)`
- Returns HTTP 200 with final state

Register both under `/api/v1/integrations/make/social`.

Test requirements:
- Integration tests: invalid Make key → 401, valid claim → 200, claim on published target → 409, valid result SUCCESS → 200 post status cascaded, result FAILED retry_count < 3 → 200 target reset
- File: `apps/api/src/__tests__/routes/integrations/make/social/callbacks.test.ts`

subtasks.title: Register routes and write integration tests

tasks.title: Create social-publish-dispatch cron job
tasks.description:
Create `apps/api/src/cron/jobs/social-publish-dispatch.job.ts`.

Job logic:
1. Log start with `@repo/logger`
2. Read `make_webhook_url` from `social_settings` (live read, not cached). If empty or placeholder: log WARN 'Make webhook URL not configured, skipping dispatch'; record in cron_runs with status=SKIPPED; return.
3. Call `SocialPublishDispatchService.findEligibleTargets()`
4. For each eligible target: call `SocialPublishDispatchService.dispatchTarget(target.id)` sequentially (not concurrent — to avoid race conditions with the DB optimistic lock)
5. Log completion with count of dispatched/failed/skipped
6. Write to `cron_runs` table with `jobName = 'social-publish-dispatch'`, `status` (COMPLETED/FAILED), `itemsProcessed` count

Schedule: every 5 minutes by default; configurable via `social_settings.dispatch_cron_interval_minutes`.

Register the job in `apps/api/src/cron/jobs/index.ts`.

Test requirements:
- Unit tests: empty make_webhook_url → job skips, no eligible targets → 0 dispatched, dispatch error is caught per-target (one failure doesn't stop others)
- File: `apps/api/src/__tests__/cron/social-publish-dispatch.test.ts`

tasks.title: Write integration test: full end-to-end GPT draft → approve → mark-ready → cron dispatch → Make claim → Make result → PUBLISHED
tasks.description:
Write a complete end-to-end integration test covering the full social automation pipeline:
1. Ingest draft via SocialDraftIngestionService (with mocked Cloudinary upload)
2. Approve via SocialPostService.approve()
3. Mark ready via SocialPostService.markReady()
4. Run findEligibleTargets() — verify target appears
5. Dispatch target via SocialPublishDispatchService.dispatchTarget() (mock Make webhook HTTP call → return 200)
6. Verify target.status = PUBLISHING, publish_log with RETRYING
7. Simulate Make claim callback via handleMakeCallbackClaim()
8. Simulate Make result SUCCESS callback via handleMakeCallbackResult()
9. Verify: target.status = PUBLISHED, post.status = PUBLISHED, audit log has TARGET_PUBLISHED entry, next_run_at = null (ONCE recurrence)
10. Verify: run with WEEKLY recurrence → post.status reset to APPROVED, next_run_at is next occurrence, all targets reset to APPROVED with retry_count=0

Test requirements:
- Full integration test with test DB
- File: `packages/service-core/src/services/social/__tests__/integration/full-pipeline.integration.test.ts`
- Mock external HTTP calls (Make webhook, Cloudinary) with vitest mocks

tasks.title: Write unit tests for dispatch cron edge cases (Phase 4 unit test coverage)
tasks.description:
Supplement T-049 with focused unit tests for the dispatch cron's edge cases that are hard to test in the integration test:
- Cron runs while post is paused → target excluded from eligible set
- Cron runs while target already in PUBLISHING → target excluded
- Cron runs with a target where retry_count = 2 (one more retry left) → dispatches, failure → retry_count becomes 3 but target stays APPROVED
- Cron runs with a target where retry_count = 3 → first check: this target should already be FAILED so excluded from eligible query
- All targets complete with mixed PUBLISHED/FAILED → post=PUBLISHED (partial success per cascade rule)
- All targets FAILED → post=FAILED, next_run_at=null (no rearm)

Test requirements:
- File: `packages/service-core/src/services/social/__tests__/dispatch-edge-cases.test.ts`

tasks.title: Add Sentry feature tag and final integration verification
tasks.description:
Final integration and cleanup tasks:
1. Tag all social route errors with Sentry feature tag `{ feature: 'social-automation' }`. Find each social route file and add `Sentry.setTag('feature', 'social-automation')` in the error handler. Follow the existing Sentry tagging pattern from other routes.
2. Verify `pnpm build:api` succeeds with all new routes registered
3. Verify `pnpm typecheck` passes across all packages
4. Run `pnpm lint` and fix any biome violations
5. Run the full test suite (`pnpm test`) and verify all new tests pass

Test requirements:
- This task is itself a quality-gate task; no new test files
- All tests from T-001 through T-051 must pass
